patch.value: Convert docs/examples extensions to TypeScript-first (starter-extension, policy-restricted-extension)

Honor ADR pm-2c28 for the committed docs/examples extensions, which are still authored in plain JavaScript (docs/examples/starter-extension/index.js 199L all-9-capabilities showcase + docs/examples/policy-restricted-extension/index.js). Add index.ts source authored against the SDK types, keep the compiled index.js the loader/tests import (mirror first-party packages/pm-*), and wire type-checking (add docs/examples/**/*.ts to a packages-style tsconfig include). The starter-extension test docstring already (incorrectly) claims TS authorship. Deferred from the scaffolder PR to keep that PR focused on the generator + SDK primitives + docs reframe.

author: codex
message: Reparent deferred docs/examples TypeScript conversion follow-up out from closed pm-09rh; pm-09rh implementation PR covered SDK/scaffold primitives, while this remains open follow-up work.

patch.value:
## Implementation (2026-06-24)

Converted both committed docs/examples reference extensions to TypeScript-first authoring, honoring ADR pm-2c28 and mirroring the first-party `packages/pm-*` pattern (TS source → committed compiled `.js` entry the loader imports).

### Source authored in TypeScript
- `docs/examples/starter-extension/index.ts` — full 9-capability showcase (commands, parser, preflight, services, renderers, hooks, schema, importers/exporters, search). Typed against `@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk`: a single `activate(api: ExtensionApi)` annotation lets every nested handler's `context` be inferred from the SDK registration contracts.
- `docs/examples/policy-restricted-extension/index.ts` — minimal command/hook/service governance demo.
- Both compiled co-located to the committed `./index.js` entry (clean `tsc` ES2022/NodeNext output, identical runtime logic; 4-space indentation matches the first-party package `.js` artifacts).

### Type-check wiring
- New `tsconfig.examples.json` (packages-style: `extends ./tsconfig.json`, `noEmit`, `include docs/examples/**/*.ts`) with a `paths` map (`@unbrained/pm-cli/sdk` → `./src/sdk/index.ts`) so the bare published specifier type-checks against source independent of a built `dist/` — the type-check analog of the existing vitest resolve alias.
- `package.json` `typecheck` script now runs the third project: `… && tsc -p tsconfig.examples.json`.

### Coverage model (aligned with packages)
- The reference extensions' compiled `.js` is now a runtime artifact, not a coverage target — exactly like `packages/**/*.js`. Coverage tracks only the `.ts` source.
- `vitest.config.ts` coverage include: dropped `docs/examples/**/*.js` (kept `.ts` + the embedding/contract-consumer `.mjs`).
- Coverage specs now import the `.ts` source (`tests/unit/docs-examples/{starter,policy-restricted}-extension.spec.ts`); the migration-string check reads the `.ts` source of truth.
- Updated the coverage-include contract test (`release-readiness-runtime.spec.ts`) to treat example `.js` as an artifact (drop it from `sourceFiles` + the expected include list), matching the existing packages treatment.

### Latent bug fixed by the type-first conversion
- The policy example's `output_format` service was `(payload) => payload`, but the real `ServiceOverride` signature passes a `ServiceOverrideContext`. Corrected to `(context) => context.payload` (identity formatter) and updated its test to pass `{ payload: … }`. Verified via the real-loader smoke that `pm policy demo` now renders the result payload correctly.

### Verification
- `pnpm typecheck` (3 projects) green.
- Full `pnpm test:coverage` green: 4989 tests / 248 files, 100% statements/branches/functions/lines.
- `pnpm quality:static`, `pnpm contracts:check`, `pnpm security:scan`, `pnpm version:check` all green.
- Manual end-to-end smoke in a temp pm project (real loader, SDK self-link): both extensions activate with `failed_count: 0`; `pm starter ping --name agent` → `starter_service_output hello=agent command=starter ping`; `--uppercase` → `hello=CLAUDE`; `pm policy demo` → `{ ok: true, command: "policy demo", source: "policy-restricted-extension" }`.

This was the last open child of epic pm-ugqx (Extension & package platform maturity).

Converted both docs/examples reference extensions (starter-extension, policy-restricted-extension) to TypeScript-first authoring: index.ts source against @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk compiled to the committed index.js entry; added tsconfig.examples.json type-check project; aligned coverage to track .ts source (compiled .js treated as runtime artifact like packages); fixed the policy output_format service signature. 100% coverage (4989 tests), all gates green, real-loader smoke verified.

Converted both docs/examples reference extensions (starter-extension, policy-restricted-extension) to TypeScript-first authoring: index.ts source against @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk compiled to the committed index.js entry; added tsconfig.examples.json type-check project; aligned coverage to track .ts source (compiled .js treated as runtime artifact like packages); fixed the policy output_format service signature. 100% coverage (4989 tests), all gates green, real-loader smoke verified.
docs/examples extensions authored TypeScript-first (index.ts source compiled to committed index.js), type-checked and coverage-tracked like first-party packages, with no runtime regression.

Both examples converted; tsconfig.examples.json type-check project added; coverage tracks the .ts source; policy service signature corrected; 100% coverage (4989 tests) and all gates green; real-loader smoke confirmed pm starter ping and pm policy demo work.

## Superseded (2026-06-24) by [[pm-2p7a]] / [[pm-m1uz]]

This task converted the docs/examples reference extensions to TypeScript-first authoring with a **committed compiled `./index.js`** entry. That compiled-`.js` model is now superseded by the TypeScript-only loading model ([[pm-m1uz]], implemented by [[pm-2p7a]]): the examples' manifest `entry` points at `./index.ts` and the loader imports it directly via Node native type stripping — the committed `index.js` artifacts were removed. The TypeScript-first authoring this task delivered stands; only "compiled to a committed `index.js`" was replaced by "the `.ts` is the entry".
